h1: How to Modify Authorized Share Capital of a Company

blocks.text: How to Modify Authorized Share Capital of a Company

The share capital of a company is a part of capital which is raised through issue of shares. A company can raise the capital up to the limit of authorized share capital, which is prescribed in the Memorandum of Association. A company can increase the Authorized Share Capital at any time after incorporation by payment of additional fee and stamp duty. To modify the clause related to Authorized Share Capital at Memorandum of Association, company has to conduct a board meeting and pass a resolution for modify authorized share capital. Clause at the MoA has to be change accommodating the change in Authorized Share Capital and the same should be approved from Ministry of Corporate Affairs.
Fill the details below for a callback.
Benefits of Increasing Authorized Share Capital

If a company planning for a business expansion or introducing a new business activity may require additional capital. In such cases, if the paid up capital reached the limit of Authorized Share Capital or the difference in the Authorized Share Capital and Paid up Capital wouldn’t meet the requirement of the situation, the company have to increase the Authorized Share Capital.Show Less

A company with good financials will always find interests investors. With the higher Authorized Share Capital, the value of the company as well rises, which supports the borrowing capacity of the company.Show Less

A company only can rise funds through shares up to the limit of Authorized Share Capital prescribed in the MoA and in the cause of demand of more capital, the company has to increase the limit of Authorized Share Capital and as per new limit, can distribute the share among the shareholders.Show Less
Documents Required for Increase Authorized Share Capital
Copy of latest amended MoA and AoA
Copy of PAN Card of the Company
Types of Share Capital in Companies
It is the maximum amount of capital a company may raise
It is that part of authorized capital, which is issued to raise capital
It is that part of issued capital, which is subscribed to be paid by the shareholders
It is that part of capital, which is subscribed and paid to company by the shareholders

items.q: 1. Which approvals are required for increase in share capital of a Private Limited Company?
items.a: Answer:The increase in authorized share capital requires:Board approval;Member approval; andRegistrar of Companies (RoC) approval. Accendoz

items.q: 2. Whether the increase of capital is reflected on the MCA portal?
items.a: Answer:Yes. The updated total authorized and paid-up capital is displayed in the company’s Master Data on the MCA portal. Accendoz

items.q: 3. When should the forms be filed with MCA?
items.a: Answer:Forms must be filed within 30 days of obtaining shareholder approval. This involves filing MGT-14 for the resolution and SH-7 along with the amended MoA/AoA. Accendoz

items.q: 4. How does the increase in capital impact the company in filing fee?
items.a: Answer:The government fee for MCA e-forms depends on authorized capital; increasing this limit will increase filing fees slightly. Accendoz
